opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I was just sitting reading, and suddenly my heart felt like a machine gun in my chest, and I feel quite dizzy.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: A sudden, very fast, regular racing heartbeat that started at rest over 30 minutes ago and has not stopped, with dizziness.

symptom_narrative.duration: this episode has lasted over 30 minutes and has not self-resolved
symptom_narrative.character: a very fast, regular pounding in the chest like a machine gun
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: distressing and frightening, with dizziness and mild breathlessness on moving
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: sudden onset at rest; he has had 3-4 similar shorter episodes over the past year that stopped on their own
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far; previous episodes stopped spontaneously
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: moving around the apartment brings on mild breathlessness
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: lightheadedness/presyncope, mild exertional breathlessness, anxiety and mild sweating; denies severe chest pain and denies fainting
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: 3-4 sudden racing-heart episodes over the last year, each self-terminating, but none this long
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: seated and distressed; able to converse normally

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; has had multiple coffees today while studying; no illicit stimulant use

psychosocial_profile.hidden_agenda: frightened by the sustained racing heart and being alone

communication_style.typical_message_style: anxious but coherent, describing the sudden onset clearly

objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: patient-counted pulse during the episode; regular and very fast
